question_text:
Subsets

Given an integer array nums of unique elements, return all possible subsets (the power set).

The solution set must not contain duplicate subsets. Return the solution in any order.
input_format: A single line of space-separated integers.
output_format: The count of all possible subsets (2^n).
constraints: All the numbers of nums are unique.
explanation: The total number of subsets for an array of n elements is always 2^n. This is because for each element, we independently choose to include or exclude it. We verify correctness by generating all subsets using backtracking: start with an empty subset, and at each step either include or skip the current element. The count of generated subsets serves as our answer. This confirms the mathematical formula while demonstrating the backtracking approach. O(N * 2^N) time, O(N) recursion space.
